entry.title: A "Quantum-Agile" Bitcoin address proposal
entry.summary:
The email from Jason to the Bitcoin Development Mailing List introduces a strategic proposal addressing the potential risks quantum computing poses to the ECDSA used in Bitcoin transactions. He suggests a flexible, user-centric approach where Bitcoin supports multiple post-quantum-secure (PSQ) algorithms instead of committing to a single solution. This method allows users to select and combine different algorithms according to their specific security needs and trust levels.
Jason outlines a system in which wallets would generate addresses by selecting public keys from one or more approved signature algorithms. These keys would be bundled, serialized, and hashed to create the address. Crucially, the complexity of the underlying algorithms remains opaque to senders, who do not need to know the specifics behind the address they are transacting with. At the point of transaction, the spender must reveal this bundle and provide valid signatures corresponding to each key it contains.
This proposed model draws inspiration from established security protocols like TLS, SSH, and IPSec, all supporting multiple algorithm suites. By adopting a similar approach, Bitcoin could benefit from several advantages. Firstly, it postpones the decision-making on which algorithm to use until more information is available, thus adapting to future developments without the need for immediate consensus changes within the community. Secondly, it facilitates quicker transitions to new algorithms in response to any cryptographic breaks or suspicions thereof. Lastly, it allows end users to tailor their security settings based on personal or transactional requirements, balancing cost against security—for example, opting for robust PSQ algorithm families with large key sizes for high-value storage or smaller keys for frequent, low-value transactions to reduce costs.
Jason has also compiled a detailed white paper elaborating on this concept, available for further review and feedback at [this link](https://zenodo.org/records/20292912). This document likely expands on the technical specifics and implementation strategies for his proposal.
